description: Confluence is a powerful tool that all organizations should leverage to maximize the knowledge sharing potential.
author: Eric T.
pros: Confluence is one of the most user-friendly content sharing tools. The flexibility of workspaces allows for organizations to build a user-friendly network of documentation. It's best feature though, is the integration with Jira. It simplifies the sharing of knowledge bases.
cons: The learning curve for permissions management can be arduous, especially when managing external accesses.

review_title: Without a doubt the no1 wiki out there.
description: Love it. Can't imagine working without it. I can dump all my work in there, invoke other users, collab on design, embed Figma and micro board on single pages. It's like my brain in one place.
author: Phil R.
pros: So fast. It's the industry standard for tech people. Personally nothing does what Confluence does. I've tried all the alternatives and nothing comes close.
cons: Other users find it a little hard at time, but with training this can be overcome. The learning curve is steep for low level users.

review_title: Easy to use for documentation, but Has Some Bugs
description: My overall experience with Confluence has been mostly positive. It’s helpful for organizing notes and working on group projects, and it’s fairly easy to use once you get used to it. There are some bugs and slow moments, but overall it gets the job done well for school work.
author: Michael M.
pros: Confluence is a good tool for keeping our documentation in a central location and feels worth the money. It’s easy to use, makes sharing of documentation easy with clients and people I work with. It has useful features, but there are some bugs at times.
cons: Some pages don’t load properly or take a long time to open. Formatting can also break, like text moving around or images not showing up right. Sometimes edits don’t save straight away, which can be frustrating. Customer support is okay, though it can be slow to reply.

review_title: A Great Resource for a Knowledge Base
description: Overall it accomplished what we were looking for, however we found the scope too narrow for what we were expecting. It served it's purpose as a tool that could capture resources and a knowledge database, but the collaborative aspect left a little to be desired for our team. For larger orgs, it may accomplish exactly what you're looking for, but for smaller, and dynamically changing orgs, it's not as intuitive as we would have liked.
author: Nolan W.
pros: It did make for an easy collaborative environment for our initial attempt at Onboarding Workflow, and allowed for an easy organization of materials, videos, documents, and workflows within our organization.
cons: It didn't allow much, at least in our application, in terms of in program collaboration. The whiteboard wasn't as intuitive as we needed for actual collaboration, and we found it easier to collaborate in a third party tool and just use Confluence as point in time references as needed.

review_title: Essential platform for technical documentation and team knowledge management
description: Overall my experience with Confluence has been very positive. At NextDay, we use it as our central hub for all technical documentation, including API contracts, front-end and back-end architecture docs, and system flow diagrams. One of the biggest advantages is the ability to integrate with developer tools via MCP or API, which allows us to create and read documentation programmatically using tools like Claude Code. Having a single, reliable place for all our technical knowledge has made collaboration and onboarding significantly easier.
author: Kaynan S.
pros: Excellent for organizing technical documentation in a structured and searchable way. The integration with developer tools through APIs and MCP makes it easy to automate document creation and keep everything up to date. The ability to document architecture, API contracts, and system flows in one place keeps the whole team aligned. Having a single source of truth for all technical knowledge has made collaboration and onboarding significantly easier at NextDay.
cons: The editor can feel a bit slow when working with large pages that contain many embedded elements. Search could also be more precise when looking for specific terms within technical documents.

description: Confluence is a solid documentation and knowledge management tool that works best when the team is disciplined about how they use it. The Jira integration and structured page organization make it a natural fit for software and project teams that need a single source of truth. However it does require upfront effort to set up properly and ongoing maintenance to keep it from becoming a dumping ground for outdated pages.
author: Rakhi V.
pros: It centralizes all project documentation in one structured space made it incredibly easy for the whole team to stay aligned without digging through emails or scattered files. The page hierarchy and space organization felt intuitive once set up, making it simple to find the right document at the right time. The integration with Jira was a genuine time saver
cons: Search functionality is another challenging point as it doesn't always surface the most relevant results, especially when pages haven't been tagged or titled consistently. Page management also becomes harder to maintain over time as spaces grow, and without strict team discipline around naming and organization, things can get messy and hard to navigate quickly.

description: I really enjoy using it, and paring it with Jira for our internal tickets it works very well together. Great to put both a Knowledge base to reference on Jira easily, and as a Work Process management, so it's very versatile.
author: Gabriel A.
pros: Confluence in my opinion, is a very good tool to keep me organized and being able to make documentation on the spot. I have used it to make How-tos and Work Process for my day to day and it was really easy to learn how to do things.
cons: Sometimes the bad part is that some of the features of their non Cloud version seemed to be better and more updated, although it does seem like they have been adding things to it as I use it.

review_title: Not a bad tool with some considerations
description: My overall experience is good. We use it in a limited form, we create a page and name it, set up if we require a tree directory so we can have sub pages and folders and build from there
author: Dan H.
pros: It's a centralized document management system that allows our team to collaborate and share knowledge to allow any of us to carry out tasks that we otherwise would not have the knowledge to do so
cons: There is a required learning curve and before a certain level of understanding is attained it can be a little difficult to get setup and going. Each page has to be unique so we sometimes need to be creative with our titles as it can't cope with duplicates under different categories, so for example Google under user process guides, you can't also have Google under say troubleshooting.

description: Overall, my experience with Confluence has been positive. It’s a reliable tool for creating, managing, and sharing documentation within a team.
author: Prachi V.
pros: Confluence has real-time editing feature is very helpful for team collaboration, and the integration with tools like Jira improves workflow efficiency.
cons: Confluence can feel slow and cluttered, especially when working with large spaces or a lot of pages. The search functionality is sometimes not very accurate,

review_title: Great tool to manage team knowledge and processes
description: Useful tool that is a vital part of our agile sprint processes and ceremonies where we can capture vital documentation easily
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Good tool for my team to capture knowledge centrally rather than keeping it in someone’s head or random doc
cons: Too many documents might make search-ability difficult but that could be due to the way we’ve organised the folders

description: Confluence is great for team documentation and collaboration, but can feel slow and cluttered without strong structure and consistent organization.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I liked how Confluence keeps team knowledge organized, searchable, and collaborative, with easy documentation, templates, and strong Jira integration for a single source of truth.
cons: I liked least that Confluence can feel slow and cluttered, with complex navigation and formatting quirks that make organizing content and finding info harder than it should be.

review_title: Confluence: Collaborative Documentation and Project Management Platform of All Times
description: All processes encompassing company files from file conversion, storage and sharing has been at breeze with Confluence.
author: Solomon I.
pros: Confluence is a crucial platform for knowledge base management, file management and project documentation. The availability of robust security features including access controls and user permissions is what I like most about Confluence.
cons: So far everything has been so good on my end when using Confluence to not only manage files and projects but also to document products.

description: Confluence is very easy to use. It makes performing your daily task easier especially when your work involves handling multiple files and resources.
author: Joseph M.
pros: Confluence makes Knowledge Management very easy especially when locating resources. Search features, File conversion and File sharing is readily available and is very useful in performing daily tasks. Issues rarely happen but customer support is very easy to contact.
cons: Making search can be difficult especially if there are a lot of resources related to your search. You have to be very specific with the search.

description: Confluence is cheap to purchase and ironically i love how it helps me gain profits and save money when making invoices and payments.
author: Norah O.
pros: I like how easy it is to share files using Confluence. Confluence has a nice customer support team that help you when you are having troubles along the way. This software is fully equipped with features that help me sort bugs and issues, so that i can work on a stress-free environment.
cons: Confluence is a tick for me and to be honest i haven't had challenges when it comes to this platform.

description: Great and reliable Product. Every QA or Software testing professional must need to adopt this powerful tool for your efforts management and time and task management to Reporting of the documents
author: Madhusudan G.
pros: As a Quality Assurance personnel, confluence playing a vital role in my day to day sprint management and report creation.
cons: The functionality to put the reports and the other side issues and task progress manage together with confluence.

review_title: Powerful Team Documentation with Strong Collaboration Features
description: works especially well for teams managing requirements, technical documentation, and shared knowledge
author: Namratha C.
pros: Its great for creating structured page, organizing content by spaces, and collaborating in real time
cons: Without strong governance and structure, spaces and pages makes hard to find the information quickly

description: Overall after 8 years with confluence my experience has been great for the most part. Highly reliable with little to no issue or downtime.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I like confluence for it's ease of use and the way you can link supporting documentation together in an easy to find fashion.
cons: The thing I would say I like least about confluence is it's search isn't always the best if you don't have things properly labeled.

description: We've been using this tool for about 10 years and it is a good tool to share information in one place. We use it to host HR policy or even pricing and promotion for marketing.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It's a great tool to document information that you need to constantly updated and then share with the team or company. It's take a bit of learning curve but once you know where everything is, it can be powerful and intuitive. There are tools to help you organize pages.
cons: It is slightly slower than google sheet and take a longer learning curve to use all the features properly.

review_title: Confluence: A Must Have Collaborative Workspace Tool for Content Sharing and Team Collaboration
description: I have been using Confluence for 5+ years now and I find it useful in documentation and collaboration.
author: Andrew M.
pros: The best aspect about Confluence is that it is an open source program. It enables us (teams) to seamlessly collaborate and share content from one central repository.
cons: Now that I am used to Confluence, I do not find it overwhelming to navigate the features.

author: Anitha R.
pros: I particularly appreciate the ability to create standardised page templates, as this helps maintain consistency across procedures, meeting notes and technical documents. Collaboration is also seamless: several people can work on the same page, add comments, or track the revision history. Finally, the integration with Jira is a real strength, as it directly links documentation with task tracking.
cons: When the volume of pages becomes significant, some information can end up being lost or duplicated if the structure is not properly managed. In such cases, it is easy to have multiple versions of the same document, which can create confusion. I also find that notifications can become excessive when following several spaces at once, and some important updates may go unnoticed.

description: I started using confluence few years ago and its been a good experience so far and would recommend it to new users.
author: saheed A.
pros: I started using confluence 3 years ago and its been mostly good experience. There are several features that are mostly of specific interest. For example, I like the ease to share document to wide range of people, export document as PDF and its general user interface
cons: Despite several useful features of Confluence, its sometimes slow. Also, its generated PDF are somewhat not of good quality. In addition, the user interface could be improved and more easily accessible because there are sometimes too many clicks and too rigid in some options in my opinion.

description: I am taking my notes on a daily basis with it. I am tracking my actions and the actions of all the team that I am working with. Creating tables with all the different data that I need to populate with other members of the company. We are also using spaces for putting our ideas and think about it. We use it for also doing some WBS or other working schemes that are used on a daily basis.
author: Antoine Q.
pros: This is a really powerful tool for taking notes, sharing datas, creating whiteboards, having box of ideas, create WBS, tracking your actions etc. This is a really easy tool proposng some basc templates and you can also use it in a more powerful sense with a bit more digging in the tool.
cons: Sometimes the website is not the most attractive one, for example, the action tracker is really ugly. It could be nice also to have a data base that you can search of what are your possibilities on a page.

description: Getting a single source of truth for all operations and compliance related documents through Confluence. It provides a lot of clarity and makes on-boarding much smoother.
author: Tim A.
pros: I enjoy being able to document processes, SOPs, and meeting notes all in one place. It’s simple to connect pages and keep things organized, especially for cross-functional projects.
cons: Search is no good unless you know the exact thing to type. And it can also become a muddled mess fairly quickly if teams don’t adhere to that same structure.

review_title: An Easy To Use Platform For Knowledge Management
description: Am happy to have known Confluence because it gets me better than other platforms. Confluence is fully packed with amazing features that simplify things for me.
author: Saleem H.
pros: Confluence gets the work done in a fantastic way. Confluence is an open and shared workspace, and that's why I love Confluence. It is easy to use Confluence. It is so easy to integrate Confluence with other platforms like Slack.
cons: Confluence is fine and doesn't have any issues.

review_title: Confluence with the Great Feature Influence
description: After 3 years of using Confluence, I find it to be very useful for many uses, including operations and engineering. Satisfactory indeed.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Confluence offers plenty of document-editing features that allow members to customize the articles according to their needs. Versatility is the forte here.
cons: Confluence would be more powerful and useful if there are advanced features to cross-reference links and utilize automation. Easier ways to organize documents would be better as well.
